surface.name: Pre-recorded transcription via cURL HTTP API (API key + free trial credits)
surface.selection_basis: The AssemblyAI Quickstart documents Using the HTTP API directly with cURL: authorization header (no Bearer prefix), POST /v2/transcript, then poll GET /v2/transcript/{id} until completed. Support docs state a limited free trial includes pre-recorded transcription with $50 of audio processing. An SDK install is not required.

alternatives_considered.name: Python/JavaScript SDK Quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: SDK wraps upload, submit, and polling; the same page documents equivalent plain HTTP/cURL.

alternatives_considered.name: AssemblyAI Playground
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: No-code try path; not the programmatic API first success.

alternatives_considered.name: Sync STT short-file API
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Separate sync endpoint requiring a local short WAV; the main Quickstart centers async /v2/transcript with a hosted sample URL.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create an AssemblyAI account and API key, then transcribe the hosted sample over HTTPS without installing an SDK.
assumed_prior_state: No AssemblyAI account, API key, or transcript is assumed.
A terminal with cURL is available; jq is helpful for the documented poll example. No language SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Completed transcript text from HTTP submit + poll
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Authenticated cURL submit and poll against https://api.assemblyai.com/v2/transcript return completed transcript text for the hosted wildfires sample.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: GET /v2/transcript/{id} returns status completed and text beginning with the wildfires sample content.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Using the HTTP API directly documents POST /v2/transcript, poll until completed, then print transcript text with cURL/jq for https://assembly.ai/wildfires.mp3.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The first meaningful STT success is completed transcript text. The Quickstart documents HTTP/cURL as a peer to the SDK. Free trial credits cover pre-recorded transcription; a credit card is the upgrade action after the $50 free limit.

prerequisites.requirement: An AssemblyAI account that can open the dashboard API Keys page.
Usable free-trial audio processing credits (up to $50). Adding a credit card upgrades after the free limit; it is not required for the first free-trial call.
An API key sent in the authorization header (no Bearer prefix).
A terminal with cURL (and jq for the documented poll loop). No language SDK is required.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to an AssemblyAI account.
details: Signup offers Google or email routes.
primary_path.input: Intent to use the AssemblyAI API
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Exact signup field transitions are not fully enumerated publicly.

primary_path.action: Confirm free-trial credits remain for pre-recorded transcription (or add a card and funds if the free $50 limit is exhausted).
details: Support FAQ: free trial includes pre-recorded transcription and up to $50 of audio processing.
Negative balance returns an API error requiring top-up.
primary_path.success_signal: Billing shows remaining free-trial balance or positive funded balance.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Exhausted free credits without upgrade block further transcription.

primary_path.action: Create or copy an API key from the dashboard and store it securely.
primary_path.success_signal: The key value is available locally.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No manual approval wait is documented for key creation.

primary_path.output: Key available to HTTP clients in the shell
primary_path.success_signal: The environment variable is set for the current shell.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Shell persistence depends on the OS.

primary_path.action: Submit a transcript with cURL POST to https://api.assemblyai.com/v2/transcript, then poll GET until status is completed.
details: audio_url: https://assembly.ai/wildfires.mp3 as in the Quickstart HTTP example.
Poll every few seconds until status is completed or error.
primary_path.success_signal: status is completed and text is present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Polling continues while processing; error status or negative balance stops the path.

primary_path.action: Confirm the transcript text is printed from the completed JSON.
primary_path.success_signal: Transcript text for the wildfires sample is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing text or error status means the first call did not succeed.

branches.condition: The developer prefers the official SDK.
branches.path: Install assemblyai for Python or JavaScript and use the one-call Transcriber path for the same hosted audio.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds package install; endpoint and key requirements stay the same.

branches.path: Add a credit card and top up on the Billing page before further API use.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Payment becomes required after free credits are gone.

friction_gates.description: Free trial covers first transcription; credit card top-up is required after the $50 free processing limit.
authorization header with the API key is required on submit and poll requests.
Async transcription requires polling until status is completed.

sources.title: Quickstart - Transcribe a pre-recorded audio file
Can I sign up for free?
sections_used: What happens when you exceed the free tier limit?
evidence_supported: Credit card upgrade / top-up after limit

uncertainties.question: Exact email/Google signup field sequence after the first provider choice.
uncertainties.impact: Account-step field inventory stays coarse for demos.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Public signup shell does not enumerate every downstream registration state.

excluded_after_success: Speaker labels and language detection options
Paid top-up beyond free-trial first success
